data.title: France hit with debt warning after surprise S&P downgrade
data.description:
S&P Global Ratings downgraded France’s long-term credit rating from AA- to A+, with a stable outlook.
source: https://www.cryptopolitan.com/france-hit-with-surprise-sp-debt-downgrade/

data.title: Arthur Hayes Family Office Plans $250 Million Fund for Mid-Sized Crypto Firms
data.description: According to PANews, Arthur Hayes' family office, Maelstrom, is preparing to raise at least $250 million to establish its first private equity fund focused on mid-sized cryptocurrency companies. The fund aims to invest between $40 million and $75 million per transaction, targeting the acquisition of up to six companies. The primary focus will be on service providers, including those offering trading infrastructure and analytics platforms.

data.title: Ondo Finance writes to SEC, calling for a stay on Nasdaq's tokenized securities plan
data.description: Ondo Finance has submitted a letter to the U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission (SEC) requesting a delay or denial of approval for Nasdaq's proposal for tokenized securities trading. Ondo argues that the plan relies on undisclosed settlement details, potentially giving large institutions an unfair advantage. In the letter, Ondo argues that regulators and investors cannot fairly evaluate the proposal without understanding how the Depository Trust Company (DTC) will handle blockchain settlement. While supporting the tokenization trend, Ondo believes approval should be suspended until the DTC system is fully developed to ensure fairness and transparency. (Cointelegraph)

data.title: Ondo Finance Urges SEC to Delay Nasdaq's Tokenized Securities Proposal
data.description: According to PANews, Ondo Finance has called on the U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission (SEC) to postpone or reject Nasdaq's proposal to trade tokenized securities, citing concerns over transparency and potential unfair advantages for existing market participants. Ondo, a blockchain company that issues tokenized versions of traditional assets, expressed in a letter to regulators that without public details on how the Depository Trust Company (DTC) will handle blockchain settlements, both regulators and investors cannot fairly assess Nasdaq's proposal. The DTC, a primary depository for U.S. securities, is responsible for facilitating post-trade settlements. Ondo highlighted that Nasdaq's regulations cannot be implemented until the DTC finalizes its system, suggesting that delaying approval until more functionalities are released is reasonable. The company urged the SEC to prioritize "open collaboration and transparency standards" before making a final decision.

data.title: UK to Establish Stablecoin Regulations by End of 2026
data.description: According to PANews, the United Kingdom is set to develop regulations for stablecoins by the end of 2026. Consultations on stablecoin regulation are scheduled to begin on November 10. Sources indicate that there is tension between the Bank of England and the government regarding this issue. The Bank of England aims to align its framework with the implementation of U.S. regulations.

Li is joining forces with several of […]

data.title: Markets are increasing bets on three Fed rate cuts this year
data.description: According to CME's "Fed Watch," the probability of a 25 basis point rate cut in October is 96.3%, and the probability of a 50 basis point cut is 3.7%. The probability of a cumulative 25 basis point rate cut in December is 0%, the probability of a 50 basis point cut is 85%, the probability of a 75 basis point cut is 14.6% (the probability was 0% yesterday), and the probability of a 100 basis point cut is 0.4%.

data.title: Cantor Fitzgerald is pushing Tether to raise about $15 billion from investors
data.description: Cantor Fitzgerald, a US financial services firm, is pushing Tether Holdings to raise approximately $15 billion from investors, valuing the company at $500 billion, comparable to OpenAI. A year ago, Cantor Fitzgerald purchased a 5% stake in Tether for over $600 million in convertible bonds. If Tether meets its fundraising target, Cantor's stake could be worth $25 billion.

data.title: Visa: Stablecoins may reshape the global $40 trillion credit market
data.description: According to Visa's latest report, stablecoins have facilitated approximately $670 billion in loans over the past five years, with the average loan amount increasing from $76,000 to $121,000. USDC and USDT account for 98% of this, consistent with their $307 billion market share. Visa notes that stablecoins have the potential to transform the credit landscape by enabling traditional financial institutions to migrate portions of the $40 trillion global credit market to programmable blockchain systems. However, the International Monetary Fund (IMF) warns that the rapid development of stablecoins could lead to increased leverage, risk accumulation, and maturity mismatches in the financial system. The Visa report emphasizes that banks and financial institutions should understand how programmable money can reshape the credit market to seize potential opportunities.

data.title: CME plans to launch sports events and economic indicators contracts to compete with Kalshi
data.description: CME Group plans to launch financial contracts tied to sports and economic indicators by the end of the year. According to people familiar with the matter, this move will put the Chicago-based CME in direct competition with prediction market platforms like Kalshi and Polymarket. Kalshi and Polymarket have rapidly entered the financial mainstream in recent years and have the backing of advisors such as Donald Trump Jr., the son of President Trump.

data.title: Spot gold breaks through $4,300/ounce, setting a new record high
data.description: Spot gold continued to rise, breaking through $4,300 per ounce, setting a new record high, rising nearly $100 during the day, an increase of nearly 2.5%.
